$comment: Championship desk pack - Title Watch's beat. Era-voiced title-race coverage: lead changes, the pressure of leading, tightening fights, finale showdowns, clinches, crownings, and season preview/review. Ids are stable rendezvous keys prefixed 'ch.' - never renamed, never reused. Read-side only.

1960s: The championship table, duly amended, accompanies this dispatch.
The scorers have re-drawn the table, and it makes instructive reading.
1980s: CHECK THE TABLE. Then check it again.
The points do not lie, and this morning they are shouting.
smgp: THE BIG BOARD RECALCULATES. EVERY PIXEL OF IT MATTERS.
SCORE UPDATE LOGGED. THE LADDER REMEMBERS EVERYTHING.
default: The table does not editorialize; it simply keeps the score.
Points are the only opinion poll this sport recognizes.
The standings column is where every argument goes to be settled.

1960s: The man at the head of the table sleeps no better for it, the old hands insist.
Leading a championship, the veterans say, is a burden dressed as an honour.
2010s: Every session now comes with a live gap graphic and a million second-guesses.
The leader's mistakes trend within minutes. That is the tax on top spot.
smgp: TOP OF THE LADDER MEANS EVERY CHALLENGER SEES YOUR NAME FIRST.
THE CROWN IS HEAVY AND THE TIMER NEVER STOPS.
default: Leading a championship is the loudest quiet job in sport.
The front of the table is the one place where every result gets measured twice.
Pressure never shows on a points table, only on the people carrying it.

1960s: The title reckoning has altered, as the tables published herewith attest.
A good deal of arithmetic was done in the paddock before the lamps went out.
1980s: The title race just changed gear.
Somebody's championship maths got torn up today.

templates.headline: First blood: {subject} leads the championship from day one
templates.deck: The season opener installs an early leader
templates.summary: {subject} left the opening round at the top of the standings.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} One round in, one name on top: {subject} leaves the season opener holding the championship lead[[?venue: after the meeting at {venue}]].
sections.context: Opening-day leads are written in pencil - there is a whole calendar left to erase them. But every title run in history began with a first week on top, and {subject} has claimed this one[[?gap: , {gap} points to the good]].
sections.next: The season will decide whether this was an omen or an accident of the calendar. {pool:chTable}

templates.headline: {subject} hits the front of the title race
templates.deck: Round {round} redraws the top of the table
templates.summary: {subject} took over the championship lead after round {round}.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} This lead was not handed out with the season's first trophies - it was earned the long way, round by round. After round {round}[[?venue: at {venue}]], the championship belongs, for now, to {subject}.
sections.championship: Mid-season leads carry a weight that opening-day leads do not: the sample is real, the rivals are known, and the arithmetic has stopped being theoretical[[?gap: - the margin stands at {gap} points]]. {pool:chTable}

templates.headline: The heaviest trophy: what the lead now asks of {subject}
templates.deck: Analysis: life at the top of the standings
templates.summary: {subject} leads the championship - and leading is its own discipline.

sections.lead: Taking a championship lead is a result. Keeping one is a temperament. From round {round} onward, {subject} is racing a different sport to everyone else on the grid.
sections.context: {pool:chPressure} Chasers get to gamble; leaders have to defend. Every strategy call now carries two prices - the one on the stopwatch and the one on the table.
sections.championship: [[?gap: The cushion is {gap} points, which is exactly as comfortable as it sounds.]] {pool:chTable}
sections.close: The chasing pack understands all of this perfectly. That is rather the point.

templates.headline: {subject} assumes the head of the championship table
templates.deck: The title reckoning after round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} now heads the drivers' table.

sections.lead: It falls to this correspondent to report a change at the head of the World Championship table: {subject} now leads the reckoning after round {round}[[?venue: , the meeting at {venue} having done the arithmetic's work]].
sections.context: Those who follow the tables closely will note[[?gap: the margin of {gap} points and]] the length of season yet to run. Championships, as ever, are won in autumn and merely discussed in spring.

templates.headline: NEW LEADER: {subject} TAKES THE TOP OF THE LADDER
templates.deck: CHAMPIONSHIP LEAD ACQUIRED IN ROUND {round}
templates.summary: {subject} grabbed the championship lead.

sections.lead: THE BIG BOARD FLASHES AND THE WHOLE PADDOCK LOOKS UP: {subject} IS THE NEW CHAMPIONSHIP LEADER[[?venue: AFTER THE SHOWDOWN AT {venue}]]!
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} [[?gap: LEAD MARGIN: {gap} POINTS. ]]EVERY RIVAL ON THE LADDER NOW HUNTS ONE NAME.

templates.headline: {subject} loses the lead[[?rival: - and {rival} is the one who took it]]
templates.deck: The title fight's sharpest edge turns after round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} lost the championship lead[[?rival: to {rival}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Of all the ways to lose a championship lead, losing it to the one name that never leaves the pit board hurts most. After round {round}, {subject} looks up at the table[[?rival: and finds {rival} on top of it]].
sections.rivalry: Rivalries are scored in seasons, not weekends, and this one just swung. The reply will matter more than the blow - the whole paddock knows it, and so do the two people involved.

templates.headline: Hunted no more: {subject} returns to the chase
templates.deck: Championship watch: the lead changes address after round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} dropped off the top of the standings.

sections.lead: The strange mercy of losing a championship lead is that the target comes off your back. From round {round}, {subject} is the hunter again[[?rival: , with {rival} now carrying the weight]].
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} Chasing is the simpler job: nothing to defend, everything to take. The season will now test whether simpler is actually better.

templates.headline: LEAD LOST! {subject} DROPS FROM THE TOP
templates.deck: THE LADDER TURNS AFTER ROUND {round}
templates.summary: {subject} lost the championship lead.

sections.lead: THE BIG BOARD BLINKS AND THE NAME ON TOP CHANGES: {subject} IS OFF THE TOP STEP OF THE LADDER[[?rival: AND {rival} STANDS ON IT]].
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} NO CONTINUES SPENT YET - THE SEASON HAS ROUNDS LEFT AND THE CROWN HAS NO NAME ON IT.

templates.headline: Change at the top: {subject} now leads the championship
templates.deck: The title table turns over after round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} took over the championship lead[[?rival: from {rival}]].

sections.lead: The top line of the championship changed hands after round {round}: {subject} up[[?rival: , {rival} down]], and every line beneath them suddenly reading differently.
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} [[?gap: The new margin at the top is {gap} points.]] Leads traded this way have a habit of being traded back - the table is volatile precisely because the field is close.

templates.headline: {gap} points: the title race is now a knife fight
templates.deck: Championship watch after round {round}
templates.summary: The gap at the top of the championship is down to {gap} points.

sections.lead: Somewhere in the last few rounds the championship stopped being a procession and became a contest. {gap} points now cover the top of the table, and the calendar is running out of room for either side to breathe.
sections.stats: At this range every small number is a big one: a single retirement swings it, one wet afternoon rewrites it, one mistake pays for the other side's winter.

templates.headline: GAME ON! Title gap down to {gap}
templates.deck: Round {round}: the championship just got interesting
templates.summary: The title gap has closed to {gap} points.

sections.lead: Rip up the coronation plans - this title race is BACK ON. The gap at the top reads {gap} points after round {round}, and the back pages finally have a proper fight to sell.
sections.close: Strap in. The run-in starts now.

templates.headline: TITLE FIGHT ALERT: {gap} POINTS IN IT
templates.deck: THE METER TIGHTENS AFTER ROUND {round}
templates.summary: The championship gap is down to {gap} points.

sections.lead: WARNING: TITLE FIGHT DETECTED. THE GAP ON THE BIG BOARD IS DOWN TO {gap} POINTS AND EVERY REMAINING ROUND IS A BOSS STAGE.

templates.headline: One round. {gap} points. Everything.
templates.deck: The championship goes to the wire
templates.summary: The title will be settled at the final round, {gap} points in it.

sections.lead: Every season secretly wants to end this way, and this one gets to: a final round with the championship live and {gap} points covering the contenders[[?subject: , {subject} holding the better hand]].
sections.context: Finales compress a year into an afternoon. Every winter test, every pit call, every point given away in the spring - all of it is now either just enough or not quite.
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} There are no second chances after this one; the table that leaves the final round is the table that goes in the record books.

templates.headline: The championship goes to the final meeting
templates.deck: {gap} points separate the principals
templates.summary: The title will be decided at the last round of the season.

sections.lead: The season's long argument will be settled at the final meeting of the year. {gap} points stand between the principal contenders[[?subject: , with {subject} holding the advantage of position]], and no one in the paddock pretends to know the outcome.
sections.context: This correspondent has seen titles settled by brilliance and by broken half-shafts alike. The final round respects neither reputation nor arithmetic - only the flag.
sections.close: One afternoon of motoring now stands for the whole year's work.

templates.headline: FINAL STAGE: THE CROWN IS DECIDED AT THE LAST ROUND
templates.deck: {gap} POINTS BETWEEN GLORY AND GAME OVER
templates.summary: The championship goes to the final round with {gap} points in it.

sections.lead: THIS IS IT. FINAL ROUND. CHAMPIONSHIP LIVE. {gap} POINTS ON THE BOARD[[?subject: AND {subject} HOLDING THE HIGH GROUND]]. THE WHOLE SEASON COMES DOWN TO ONE GREEN LIGHT.
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} WINNER TAKES THE CROWN. EVERYONE ELSE TAKES THE LOADING SCREEN.

templates.headline: No contest: {subject} clinches the {year} title early
templates.deck: A championship won with rounds still on the calendar
templates.summary: {subject} sealed the {year} championship with races to spare.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The season just conceded. {subject} has clinched the {year} championship with rounds still to run[[?gap: , the gap of {gap} points now beyond mathematics]], and what remains of the calendar is a lap of honour.
sections.stats: Titles sealed early are not lucky; they are cumulative. Round after round the margin grew until the table simply ran out of counter-arguments.

templates.headline: Sealed: {subject} closes out the {year} championship
templates.deck: The title is decided before the season is done
templates.summary: {subject} is the {year} champion, confirmed with rounds remaining.

sections.lead: There was no single knockout blow - just a season of pressure applied until the arithmetic gave way. {subject} is the {year} champion, confirmed with racing still left on the calendar.
sections.context: Championships like this are built on the unglamorous weekends: the damage-limitation drives, the points salvaged from bad Saturdays[[?gap: , a margin of {gap} points assembled a handful at a time]].
sections.impact: [[?team: For {team}, the title validates every decision that looked cautious at the time.]] {pool:champChatter}

templates.headline: CHAMPION CONFIRMED: {subject} LOCKS THE CROWN EARLY
templates.deck: TITLE SECURED WITH ROUNDS TO SPARE
templates.summary: {subject} clinched the championship early.

sections.lead: STAGE CLEAR - SEASON CLEARED EARLY! {subject} HAS LOCKED THE CROWN WITH ROUNDS STILL ON THE BOARD[[?gap: , {gap} POINTS CLEAR AND MATHEMATICALLY UNTOUCHABLE]].
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} THE REMAINING ROUNDS ARE A VICTORY LAP AND EVERYBODY IN THE HALL KNOWS IT.

templates.headline: The arithmetic closes: {player's} title bid is over
templates.deck: Post-mortem on a championship that got away
templates.summary: {subject} is mathematically out of the {year} title race.

sections.lead: Championships rarely die on the day they are lost. Somewhere back down the calendar - a retirement here, a ninth place that should have been a fifth there - the {year} title quietly slipped out of reach, and after round {round} the mathematics finally said so out loud.
sections.context: The post-mortem will find no single wound. Title bids bleed out in increments, which is why the good teams grieve for exactly one evening and then start itemizing.
sections.next: What survives is the baseline: a season spent at the sharp end changes what everyone - rivals included - expects of the next one.

templates.headline: CROWN OUT OF REACH FOR {subject}
templates.deck: TITLE RUN ENDS - THE LADDER GOES ON
templates.summary: {subject} can no longer win the crown this season.

sections.lead: THE METER SAYS IT PLAIN: THE CROWN IS OUT OF REACH THIS SEASON. {subject} FOUGHT THE NUMBERS AND THE NUMBERS WON[[?rival: - {rival} CANNOT BE CAUGHT]].
sections.context: BUT HEAR THIS: THE LADDER KEEPS EVERY SCORE. A SEASON AT THE SHARP END IS SAVED PROGRESS, NOT A WASTED RUN.
sections.close: CONTINUE? THE ANSWER IS ALWAYS YES.

templates.headline: {subject}: World Champion. Say it slowly.
templates.deck: The {year} title belongs to the name this paper has followed all season
templates.summary: {subject} is the {year} World Champion.

sections.lead: Careers are long lists of results, and then - if absolutely everything holds - one line that outranks all of them. {subject} is the {year} World Champion, and nothing written about {player} before today reads the same way again.
sections.impact: [[?team: {team} takes its share of the glory, and every mechanic in that garage knows which title they will be telling their grandchildren about.]] The champion's name changes the paddock's whole geometry: the benchmark now has an address.
sections.close: Tonight the record book is the only journalism that matters: one season, one name, in ink.

templates.headline: The season's verdict: {subject} takes the {year} crown
templates.deck: The championship has its answer - and the rest of the grid has its target
templates.summary: {subject} won the {year} drivers' championship.

sections.lead: The {year} championship belongs to {subject}, and the rest of the field now gets a whole winter to study exactly how it happened.
sections.context: From the outside, a rival's title is a syllabus: where the points came from, which weekends were stolen rather than inherited, what it costs to sustain a season without a soft middle.
sections.next: Champions set the standard the field must answer. The first question of next season was written today, and it has {subject's} name in it.

templates.headline: CHAMPION OF THE WORLD: {subject} TAKES THE CROWN
templates.deck: THE LADDER HAS A NAME AT THE VERY TOP
templates.summary: {subject} is this season's champion.

sections.lead: CUE THE FANFARE AND ROLL THE CREDITS: {subject} IS THE CHAMPION! THE NAME GOES UP IN LIGHTS ON THE BIG BOARD AND EVERY CABINET IN THE HALL PLAYS THE VICTORY THEME.
sections.impact: [[?team: {team} PAINTS THE CROWN ON THE GARAGE DOOR. ]]FROM TOMORROW, EVERY CHALLENGER ON THE LADDER IS CLIMBING TOWARD ONE NAME.
sections.close: HIGH SCORE. NEW RECORD. SAVED FOREVER.

templates.headline: Moving up: {subject} climbs to {ord:champPosition}
templates.deck: Championship watch: the table shuffles after round {round}
templates.summary: {subject} rose to {ord:champPosition} in the standings.

sections.lead: The middle of a points table is a queue, and {subject} just moved up it: {ord:champPosition} in the championship after round {round}.
sections.stats: Climbs like this are what title challenges look like three months before anyone dares call them that - position by position, weekend by weekend, until the table has no choice.

templates.headline: {subject} CLIMBS THE LADDER TO {ord:champPosition}
templates.deck: RANK UP AFTER ROUND {round}
templates.summary: {subject} moved up to {ord:champPosition} on the ladder.

sections.lead: RANK UP! {subject} MOVES TO {ord:champPosition} ON THE LADDER AFTER ROUND {round}, AND THE NAMES ABOVE JUST FELT THE FOOTSTEPS.
sections.championship: {pool:chTable} THE ONLY DIRECTION THAT COUNTS IS UP.

templates.headline: The {year} season opens: form, machinery and expectation
templates.deck: A preview of the championship year[[?team: - {player} entered with {team}]]
templates.summary: The {year} championship season has begun.

sections.lead: The entry lists are lodged, the transporters have crossed their borders, and the {year} World Championship season stands ready. The winter's work - new machinery, new alliances, quiet defections - will now be examined in public.
sections.context: The formbook, as in every new year, is a document of faith rather than fact[[?team: . In the {team} camp the faith is that this is their year; every other camp holds the identical conviction]].
sections.next: The opening meeting will retire half the paddock's theories by Sunday evening. It always does.

templates.headline: {year} is live: everything you need before lights out
templates.deck: Season preview[[?team: - {player} starts the year at {team}]]
templates.summary: The {year} season is underway.

sections.lead: New season, clean table, zero points of separation: {year} is live. Preseason talk is now officially worthless[[?team: , and nobody at {team} is sorry about that]].
sections.context: The winter data all says the same thing: nobody knows anything until the first stint of the first race. That data point finally lands this weekend.
sections.next: First question of the year: who actually found lap time over the winter, and who found a marketing department?

templates.headline: SEASON {season} START: PRESS START TO RACE
templates.deck: A NEW CAMPAIGN LIGHTS UP THE BOARD
templates.summary: Season {season} is underway.

sections.lead: THE CABINET HUMS, THE BOARD RESETS, AND SEASON {season} BEGINS: EVERY SCORE AT ZERO, EVERY DREAM AT FULL HEALTH[[?team: , AND {player} SUITED UP FOR {team}]].
sections.next: THE LADDER IS TALL AND THE LEGEND AT THE TOP DOES NOT COME DOWN TO MEET ANYBODY. CLIMB.

templates.headline: The {year} season closes its account
templates.deck: A review of the championship year
templates.summary: The {year} season has concluded.

sections.lead: The final flag of {year} has fallen, and the season passes from the timekeepers to the historians. The tables are settled; the arguments, naturally, are not.
sections.context: It was a year, as all years are, of machinery vindicated and machinery betrayed, of reputations made at speed and unmade at leisure. The winter will re-litigate every meeting of it in the club rooms and the drawing offices alike.
sections.close: The transporters turn for home. The {year} chapter is bound and shelved.

templates.headline: SEASON {season} COMPLETE
templates.deck: RESULTS SAVED TO THE BIG BOARD
templates.summary: Season {season} is in the books.

sections.lead: FINAL FLAG. FINAL SCORE. SEASON {season} IS COMPLETE AND EVERY LINE OF IT IS BURNED INTO THE BIG BOARD FOREVER.
sections.stats: THE LADDER TAKES THE WINTER TO MEMORIZE THE NUMBERS. IT FORGETS NOTHING AND FORGIVES LESS.

templates.headline: NEW CHALLENGER: {player} ENTERS THE CHAMPIONSHIP
templates.deck: A NEW NAME JOINS THE LADDER[[?team: WITH {team}]]
templates.summary: {player} begins the climb[[?team: with {team}]].

sections.lead: A NEW CHALLENGER APPROACHES! {player} JOINS THE GRID[[?team: IN {team} COLOURS]] WITH AN EMPTY SCORE LINE AND A FULL TANK OF INTENT.
sections.context: THE LADDER DOES NOT CARE WHERE YOU CAME FROM. IT ONLY COUNTS WHAT YOU DO NEXT.
sections.close: WELCOME TO THE SHOW. THE CLOCK IS ALREADY RUNNING.
